title: Detection of Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms with Padlock Probes
author: Tomasz Krzywkowski
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wjhfcj6

steps.title: Culture cells in a flask until 80-90% confluence
steps.instruction: Culture cells in a flask until 80-90% confluence.

steps.title: Wash cells twice with PBS, and...
steps.instruction: Wash cells twice with PBS, and dislodge cells by adding 1 mL/25 cm 2 of 0.25% Trypsin-EDTA for 1-2 min. 00:01:00, Trypsin

steps.title: Resuspend the cells in appropriate culturing...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells in appropriate culturing medium (FBS in a medium inactivates the trypsin).

steps.title: Place sterile microscope slides in a...
steps.instruction: Place sterile microscope slides in a 150 mm X 25 mm culture dish and add ~22 mL of medium to cover the slides. Cells adhere to most type of slides. Note: You could also use specialized chambered culture slides that contain cell cultures to small volume directly on the slide.

steps.title: Carefully, seed a total 3 mL...
steps.instruction: Carefully, seed a total 3 mL of suspended cells directly on the slides (see Note 9). Note 9: Unlike in many in situ hybridization methods, where cells are grown or coverslips, we grow cells on the microscope slides directly. We found slides to be more resistant to breaking (especially when applying and pealing the silicone chambers off) and on the slides, multiple experiments can be run in parallel (up to eight), making the work more convenient. Special microscope culture slides can also be used in growing cells on slides. Also, multiple consecutive tissue section can be placed on a single slide to facilitate fast experimenting and imaging.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the closed dish into...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the closed dish into the incubator and incubate the cells under appropriate conditions to allow them to adhere to the microscope slides (see Note 10). Note 10: Optimal seeding conditions should be identified experimentally for every cell line. For cells with large cytoplasm, 3 mL suspension is usually enough to create homogenous cell layer on each slide. Cells with smaller cytoplasm can be seeded at higher density. In our experience, overnight incubation allows cells to adhere to slides efficiently. Extended incubation can result in cell proliferation on-slide (too dense or clumped cells are difficult to analyze by image analysis software).

steps.title: Wash the slides twice with cold...
steps.instruction: Wash the slides twice with cold PBS and transfer the slides to a sterile Coplin jar or sterile slides transport box.

steps.title: Fix the cells with 3
steps.instruction: Fix the cells with 3.7% formaldehyde at room temperature (RT) for 20 min. 00:20:00, Fixation Safety precautions: Formaldehyde

steps.title: Discard formaldehyde appropriately and wash the slides twice with PBS
steps.instruction: Discard formaldehyde appropriately and wash the slides twice with PBS.

steps.title: Gradually dehydrate and pre-permeabilize the cells...
steps.instruction: Gradually dehydrate and pre-permeabilize the cells in ethanol series (70, 85, and 99.5% each step for 3 min). 00:03:00, 70% EtOH 00:03:00, 85% EtOH 00:03:00, 99.5% EtOH

steps.title: Air dry the slides
steps.instruction: Air dry the slides. For future use, slides can be stored at -80°C (long-term storage) or -20°C (up to 2 weeks). Make sure slides are labeled appropriately. For stored frozen slides, allow to thaw to RT before continuing.

steps.title: Attach Secure-Seal chamber of appropriate size...
steps.instruction: Attach Secure-Seal chamber of appropriate size and rehydrate the cells by adding PBS-T into the chamber (see Note 11). Note 11: Tween 20, as a surfactant, coats the chambers, facilitates buffer exchange and prevents formation of "dead spaces” inside the chamber. As a detergent, it can provoke bubble formation. We recommend adding a buffer into a chamber when slide is slightly tilted.

steps.title: Remove PBS-T and permeabilize the cells by adding 0
steps.instruction: Remove PBS-T and permeabilize the cells by adding 0.1 M HCl for 5 min. 00:05:00, 0.1 M HCl Safety precautions: Hydrochloric acid

steps.title: Remove HCl and wash the cells twice with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Remove HCl and wash the cells twice with PBS-T

steps.title: Tissue is cryosectioned and mounted on...
steps.instruction: Tissue is cryosectioned and mounted on microscope slides (see Note 12) and can be stored at -80°C until use. Note 12: Any slides that enhance adhesion of tissue sections can be used. (SuperFrost Plus® from Menzel-Glaser work very well in our hands). Sections should be as thin as possible (preferably few cell layer). Thinner sections are more prone to break and fold during cutting. We commonly use 10 μm thick sections.

steps.title: Thaw slides at RT for a few minutes
steps.instruction: Thaw slides at RT for a few minutes. 00:03:00, RT Thaw

steps.title: Fix the tissue in 3
steps.instruction: Fix the tissue in 3.7% formaldehyde for 45 min. Use a sterile Coplin Jar or perform fixation directly on tissue sections on flat laying slides. 00:45:00, Fixation Safety precautions: Formaldehyde

steps.title: Wash once with PBS for 5 min
steps.instruction: Wash once with PBS for 5 min. 00:05:00, PBS wash

steps.title: (Optional) Permeabilize the tissue by incubating...
steps.instruction: (Optional) Permeabilize the tissue by incubating with prewarmed pepsin in 0.1 M HCl at 37°C. Digestion with 250 U/mL (0.1 mg/mL) pepsin for 5 min is a good starting point in our experience. Optimal conditions need to be identified for the respective specimen (see Notes 4 and 13). 00:05:00, HCl 37 °C Safety precautions: Hydrochloric acid Note 4: Lyophilized pepsin batches may vary in activity, even from the same supplier. Every new batch of pepsin should be tested on established model. We typically detect housekeeping gene (ACTB or GAPDH) in the same tissue type to evaluate detection reproducibility. Note 13: The fixation time needs to compromise optimal fixative diffusion and minimize loss of tissue content. We recommend pragmatic evaluation of fixation parameters (consecutive sections should be used for each condition). Fixation time may vary for different tissues and different specimen thicknesses. Housekeeping gene is a good candidate for such optimization studies. Use conditions showing maximal signal amount.

steps.title: Wash once with PBS for 5 min
steps.instruction: Wash once with PBS for 5 min. 00:05:00, Wash

steps.title: Dehydrate the tissue section in the...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate the tissue section in the ethanol series (70, 85, and 99.5% ethanol, 3 min each). 00:03:00, 70% EtOH 00:03:00, 85% EtOH 00:03:00, 99.5% EtOH

steps.title: Let slide air dry and mount appropriate size Secure-Seal chamber
steps.instruction: Let slide air dry and mount appropriate size Secure-Seal chamber.

steps.title: Rehydrate the tissue with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Rehydrate the tissue with PBS-T.

steps.title: Combine reagents in table below and...
steps.instruction: Combine reagents in table below and then remove PBS-T from chamber and apply the RT mix to the chamber.µ %s %s

steps.title: Cover the chamber inlets and incubate the slides at 37°C
steps.instruction: Cover the chamber inlets and incubate the slides at 37°C. The optimal incubation time needs to be determined experimentally. With LNA-modified target-specific primers, 1 hour is sufficient. 37 °C 01:00:00, LNA primers With random decamers, reverse transcription is typically conducted overnight. 37 °C Decamers overnight.

steps.title: Wash the chamber twice with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Wash the chamber twice with PBS-T.

steps.title: Combine reagents in the table below...
steps.instruction: Combine reagents in the table below for the padlock probe ligation mix. %s %s Safety precautions: Formamide

steps.title: Remove PBS-T and add combined ligation...
steps.instruction: Remove PBS-T and add combined ligation mix reagents to the chamber. Cover the chamber inlets and incubate the slides in a humidity chamber at 37°C for 30 min. 37 °C 00:30:00, PLP hybridization (1)

steps.title: Transfer humidity chamber to 45°C incubator for 45 min
steps.instruction: Transfer humidity chamber to 45°C incubator for 45 min. (see Note 14) 45 °C 00:45:00, PLP hybridization (2) Note 14: RNase H has the highest activity at 37°C. It degrades RNA from mRNA/cDNA heteroduplex during the first 37°C incubation. After 30 min, sample is transferred to 45°C which is the optimal temperature for the Tth ligase. Addition of formamide into the mix lowers dsDNA stability (Tm of PLP arms/cDNAduplex). This enables extension of PLP arms that strengthens probe “locking” on cDNA and gives a good balance between arms melting and specific binding.

steps.title: Remove reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Remove reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T.

steps.title: Combine the reagents in the table...
steps.instruction: Combine the reagents in the table below for the RCA mix. %s %s

steps.title: Remove PBS-T and add combined ligation...
steps.instruction: Remove PBS-T and add combined ligation mix reagents to the chamber. Cover the chamber inlets and incubate the slides in a humidity chamber at 37°C for ≥1 hour. (see Note 15) 37 °C 01:00:00, RCA Note 15: The optimal temperature for phi29 polymerase is 37°C. We typically conduct RCA for 1 hour. If RCA is performed for several hours (or overnight) at 37°C, RCPs may start to fragment what could interfere with accurate signal counting. If large RCPs are desired (thick tissue sections or those with high autofluorescence), we advise doing RCA at RT overnight. Such approach will generate very large but compact RCPs.

steps.title: Remove RCA reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Remove RCA reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T.

steps.title: Combine the reagents in the table...
steps.instruction: Combine the reagents in the table below for the hybridization mix. %s %s Safety precautions: Formamide

steps.title: Remove PBS-T and apply combined reagents...
steps.instruction: Remove PBS-T and apply combined reagents to chamber and incubate the slide at RT for ~20 min. (see Note 16) 00:20:00, Decorator probe Note 16: In a multiplexed reaction (when more than one decorator probe is used), we recommend incubation at 37°C for 30 min (to minimize nonspecific binding of the oligonucleotides). In such case, cover the chamber inlets to prevent mix evaporation.

steps.title: Remove reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Remove reagents and wash the chamber twice with PBS-T.

steps.title: Mark the position of the chamber...
steps.instruction: Mark the position of the chamber on the backside of the slide with a diamond pen and remove the Secure-Seal chamber. (see Note 17) Note 17: A double edge razor can be used to facilitate complete removal of the Secure-Seal chamber.

steps.title: Dehydrate the slides through an ethanol...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate the slides through an ethanol series (70, 85, and 99.5% ethanol, each for 3 min). 00:03:00, 70% EtOH 00:03:00, 85% EtOH 00:03:00, 99.5% EtOH Allow slide to air dry.

steps.title: Apply Slow-Fade medium to the speciment...
steps.instruction: Apply Slow-Fade medium to the speciment and gently, mount the specimen. (see Note 5 and 18) Note 5: Slow-Fade® Gold Antifade Mountant works best in our experience to prevent photobleaching. Note 18: We typically apply 5-7 μL of mounting medium for single, 50 μL Secure-Seal chamber. Remove excess of the medium by gently pressing the slide and coverslip against a paper towel (excess of medium will be absorbed by the paper towel).

steps.title: Sectioned tissues are mounted on microscope...
steps.instruction: Sectioned tissues are mounted on microscope slides (see Note 12) and can be stored at -80°C until use. Note 12: Any slides that enhance adhesion of tissue sections can be used. (SuperFrost Plus® from Menzel-Glaser work very well in our hands). Sections should be as thin as possible (preferably few cell layer). Thinner sections are more prone to break and fold during cutting. We commonly use 10 μm thick sections.

steps.title: Frozen samples are thawed at RT
steps.instruction: Frozen samples are thawed at RT.

steps.title: 10 min xylene 00:10:00, Xylene (
steps.instruction: 10 min xylene 00:10:00, Xylene (

steps.title: 2X 2 min 100% ethanol 00:02:00, 100% EtOH (
steps.instruction: 2X 2 min 100% ethanol 00:02:00, 100% EtOH (

steps.title: 2X 2 min 95% ethanol 00:02:00, 95% EtOH (
steps.instruction: 2X 2 min 95% ethanol 00:02:00, 95% EtOH (

steps.title: 2X 2 min 70% ethanol 00:02:00, 70% EtOH (
steps.instruction: 2X 2 min 70% ethanol 00:02:00, 70% EtOH (

steps.title: 5 min DEPC-H 2 O 00:05:00,...
steps.instruction: 5 min DEPC-H 2 O 00:05:00, H2O 5 min PBS 00:05:00, PBS Safety precautions: Xylene

steps.title: Permeabilize the tissue by incubating with pre-warmed pepsin in 0
steps.instruction: Permeabilize the tissue by incubating with pre-warmed pepsin in 0.1 M HCl at 37°C for 5 min (see Notes 4 and 13). 00:05:00, HCl 37 °C Safety precautions: Hydrochloric acid Note 4: Lyophilized pepsin batches may vary in activity, even from the same supplier. Every new batch of pepsin should be tested on established model. We typically detect housekeeping gene (ACTB or GAPDH) in the same tissue type to evaluate detection reproducibility. Note 13: The fixation time needs to compromise optimal fixative diffusion and minimize loss of tissue content. We recommend pragmatic evaluation of fixation parameters (consecutive sections should be used for each condition). Fixation time may vary for different tissues and different specimen thicknesses. Housekeeping gene is a good candidate for such optimization studies. Use conditions showing maximal signal amount.

steps.title: Wash with PBS for 5 min
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS for 5 min. 00:05:00, PBS Wash

steps.title: Postfix the tissue in 3
steps.instruction: Postfix the tissue in 3.7% formaldehyde for 10 min at RT. 00:10:00, Postfix

steps.title: Wash twice with PBS for 5 min
steps.instruction: Wash twice with PBS for 5 min. 00:05:00, PBS Wash (1) 00:05:00, PBS Wash (2)

steps.title: Dehydrate the tissue section in the...
steps.instruction: Dehydrate the tissue section in the ethanol series (70, 85, and 99.5% ethanol, each step for 3 min). 00:03:00, 70% EtOH 00:03:00, 85% EtOH 00:03:00, 99.5% EtOH

steps.title: Air dry the slides and mount Secure-Seal chambers
steps.instruction: Air dry the slides and mount Secure-Seal chambers.

steps.title: Rehydrate the tissue with PBS-T
steps.instruction: Rehydrate the tissue with PBS-T.

steps.title: Mounted slides can be imaged immediately
steps.instruction: Mounted slides can be imaged immediately. Conventional wide-field epifluorescence microscopes are usually sufficient to image RCA products in tissue sections and cells. (see Note 19) Note 19: We use a Zeiss Axioplan II Epifluorescence microscope equipped with either a metal hallide lamp or LED light source (Lumencor SpectraX) and a Hamamatsu, sCMOS camera. The following filter setup provides good wavelength separation and minimal crosstalk between different channels. 38HE (Zeiss) for imaging GFP/FITC/FAM dyes; SP102v2 (Chroma) for imaging Cy3 (minimal crass talk with 38HE filter); SP103v2 (Chroma) for imaging Cy3.5/TexasRed; SP104v2 (Chroma) for imaging Cy5; 49007 (Chroma) for imaging Cy7/Alexa 7.5 dyes. Make sure to use filters appropriate for nuclear staining and fluorophores used in the experiment. Depending on the level of detail desired, use appropriate objective (we typically use 10X, 20X and 40X high numerical aperture objectives). To ensure accurate signal quantitation during image analysis, carefully adjust exposure time for a signal channels (abundant RCPs might be hard to segment if overexposed). We typically acquire 5-10 μm thick z-stack of images to capture RCPs in all focal planes (if possible, preview the specimen to define "first" and "last" stack corresponding to RCPs present in the lowest and highest stack and capture all images within this range).

steps.title: Z-Stacks with multiple focal planes are...
steps.instruction: Z-Stacks with multiple focal planes are combined to a single 2D maximum intensity projection (MIP) image that can be used in image analysis. We recommend taking images from several positions for each experiment (depending on cell density) to account for cell-to-cell expression variations.

steps.title: Open-source programs such as CellProfiler or...
steps.instruction: Open-source programs such as CellProfiler or ImageJ can be used for image analysis. (see Note 20) Note 20: CellProfiler is a great, user-friendly tool to aid biologists in image processing and analysis. With respect to presented protocol, CellProfiler offers scripts for cell segmentation (definition of the nucleus and the cytoplasm), RCP segmentation, and assignment of RCPs to individual cells or fluorescence measurements. All scripts can be implemented in automated pipeline, allowing for batch image processing. An example script for cell and RCP identification is available at CellProfiler website http://www.cellprofiler.org. Briefly, gray scale TIFF images (offering highest resolution, JPEG images are processed faster and can also be used) from individual fluorescence channels are loaded into the pipeline. Cells are segmented to nuclei and cytoplasm and RCPs are identified and related to neighboring cells. Finally, number of RCPs for each cell is exported as a .csv file, which can be used for post-analysis processing.

steps.title: Aspirate Trypsin-EDTA and monitor cells trypsinization under a brightfield microscope
steps.instruction: Aspirate Trypsin-EDTA and monitor cells trypsinization under a brightfield microscope.

steps.title: Remove reverse transcription reagents and wash...
steps.instruction: Remove reverse transcription reagents and wash the chamber once with PBS-T.

steps.title: Every discrete, fluorescent detected signal originates...
steps.instruction: Every discrete, fluorescent detected signal originates from a single, successful detection of the SNP All RCPs should be localized to the cytoplasm, however RCPs can occasionally dissociate out of cells and generate RCPs on the glass slide (such signals should be disregarded). For anticipated results, we refer to the accompanying publiction. https://link.springer.com/protocol/10.1007%2F978-1-4939-7213-5_14

steps.title: Fix cells with 3
steps.instruction: Fix cells with 3.7% formaldehyde. (See Note 3) For cultured cells, 10 min. 00:10:00, Cell postfixation For tissue sections, up to 45 min. 00:45:00, Tissue postfixation Note 3: We recommend using freshly prepared formaldehyde solutions in DEPC-PBS. Working solutions can be prepared form either 37% methanol-stabilized stock solution or from paraformaldehyde powder. Aliquots of 3.7% formaldehyde in DEPC-PBS in 1 mL (used during the experiment) and 15 mL (for cell fixation) can be stored at -20°C. Do not freeze and thaw. Safety precautions: Formaldehyde
